title: Der Zauberberg. Zweiter Band
summaries: "Der Zauberberg. Zweiter Band" by Thomas Mann is a novel published in 1924. Young engineer Hans Castorp visits his cousin at a Swiss sanatorium in the mountains before 1914, intending to stay three weeks. Enchanted by the isolated world of illness and its inhabitants, he remains far longer. There he encounters the humanist Settembrini, who becomes his philosophical mentor, and the captivating Russian woman Madame Chauchat, who awakens a dangerous fascination. In this removed realm, Castorp confronts politics, philosophy, love, disease, and death. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
